name: Fixed-Point Iteration Combinator
description: Initialize state, apply a body step repeatedly, and check convergence after each iteration until the stopping criterion is met or the iteration budget is exhausted.
dejargonized_summary: Start with an initial guess, keep improving it one step at a time, and stop when it stops changing or you run out of tries.

inputs.name: initial_state
inputs.constraints: Seed state for the first iteration. Type depends on the problem domain.

outputs.name: converged_state
outputs.constraints: Final state after convergence or iteration budget exhaustion.

planning_constraints.subject: convergence_guarantee
planning_constraints.statement: The body_step must be contractive or the iteration budget must be finite.
planning_constraints.rationale: Without a convergence guarantee or a hard iteration limit, the loop may run indefinitely. Set fixed_point_max_iterations > 0 as a safety bound.

planning_constraints.subject: state_threading
planning_constraints.statement: The output of body_step must be type-compatible with its input for the next iteration.
planning_constraints.rationale: The iteration feeds next_state back as the input to body_step. Type mismatch between iterations will cause runtime failures.

stages.name: Body Init
stages.description: Seed the iteration state from the external input. May involve preprocessing, default initialization, or warm-starting from a prior solution.
stages.dejargonized_description: Set up the starting point for the iterative improvement process.
guarantees: Output state is well-formed for body_step.

stages.name: Body Step
stages.description: Perform one iteration step: take the current state and produce a refined next state. This is the stage the architect decomposes further — it represents the core computation that is repeated.
stages.dejargonized_description: Do one round of improvement on the current best guess.

outputs.name: next_state
outputs.constraints: Refined state after one step. Must be type-compatible with input for iteration.

guarantees: Output is a refined version of the input state.
family_notes: This stage is abstract — the architect must decompose it into concrete atoms.
Example: for unsupervised MDM refinement (Barachant DecMeg), body_step is predict-labels → retrain-subject-model → reclassify.

stages.name: Convergence Check
stages.description: Test whether the iteration should stop. Compares previous and next states to determine if the stopping criterion is met (e.g., state change below threshold, loss plateaued, max iterations reached).
stages.dejargonized_description: Check whether the improvement is small enough to stop iterating.

outputs.name: converged
outputs.constraints: True if the iteration should stop.

preconditions: Both states are available and type-compatible.

edges.provenance: Initialized state feeds into first iteration.

audit.rationale: Fixed-point iteration is a fundamental combinator needed for self-training loops (Barachant DecMeg unsupervised MDM refinement), iterative solvers, and convergence-based algorithms.
audit.dejargonized_summary: Start with an initial guess, keep improving it one step at a time, and stop when it stops changing or you run out of tries.
provenance_notes: This JSON asset replaces the Python-only definition with an auditable, versioned asset.
uncertainty_notes: The body_step stage is deliberately abstract (concept_type=custom) — the architect must decompose it per-problem.
Convergence is not guaranteed for non-contractive body steps; the max_iterations safety bound is critical.

references.title: Banach fixed-point theorem
references.note: Theoretical foundation for contraction-based convergence guarantees.

references.title: Solution CDG Gap Analysis
references.note: S-2 orchestration gap: iterative refinement patterns require FIXED_POINT combinator.
